reforms.description: Sweden implemented reforms to boost employment and labour supply, including changes to unemployment insurance and the introduction of an in-work tax credit, which are expected to reduce structural unemployment by an estimated 0.5-2.0 percentage points.
reforms.component_name: Unemployment Insurance and In-Work Tax Credit
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to reduce unemployment, thereby increasing the labour supply and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into effect and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in the labour market.
reforms.source_quote: The crisis hit as Sweden was implementing reforms to boost employment and labour supply, including a reform of unemployment insurance and the introduction of an in-work tax credit.

reforms.description: Sweden has developed an electricity certificate system to promote renewable energy production, requiring electricity producers to purchase certificates equivalent to a certain proportion of their sales, thereby creating demand for renewable energy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The system incentivizes renewable energy production, which can lead to innovation and investment in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The system is currently operational and in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new system for promoting renewable energy represents a significant policy shift.
reforms.source_quote: The electricity certificate system is the main instrument used by Sweden to develop renewables in the electricity sector.

reforms.description: The Swedish government announced new investments in renewable energy technologies and plans to support research efforts in vehicle technology with SEK450 million a year for the next five years, focusing on electric and hybrid vehicle development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in renewable energy and vehicle technology are expected to drive innovation and economic growth in these sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has publicly stated its commitment to these investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: The investments represent a significant but narrower focus on specific sectors.
reforms.source_quote: Support to research efforts in vehicle technology (SEK450 million a year for the next five years) focusing on the development of electric and hybrid vehicle technology.
